title: Longest Repeating Character Replacement
description:
Longest Repeating Character Replacement

You are given a string s and an integer k. You can choose any character of the string and change it to any other uppercase English letter. You can perform this operation at most k times.

Return the length of the longest substring containing the same letter you can get after performing the above operations.

Examples:

  • Input: s = "ABAB", k = 2
    Output: 4
    Explanation: Replace the two 'A's with two 'B's or vice versa.
  • Input: s = "AABABBA", k = 1
    Output: 4
    Explanation: Replace the one 'A' in the middle with 'B' and form "AABBBBA". The substring "BBBB" has the longest repeating letters, which is 4. There may exist other ways to achieve this answer too.

Constraints:

  • 1 <= s.length <= 105
  • s consists of only uppercase English letters.
  • 0 <= k <= s.length
solution_text:
Longest Repeating Character Replacement

Algorithm (Sliding Window with Frequency Count):

  1. Use a sliding window with left pointer l and right pointer r.
  2. Track the frequency of each character in the window.
  3. Track max_freq — the count of the most frequent character in the window.
  4. If window_size - max_freq > k, we need more than k replacements, so shrink from the left.
  5. The answer is the maximum window size seen.

Time Complexity: O(n)

Space Complexity: O(1) — at most 26 characters

Key Insight: We don't need to decrease max_freq when shrinking the window. It only needs to increase to find a longer valid window.
